title: Script P3: Open Reading Frame Prediction
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"178fh","text":"This protocol provides a method for predicting the locations of the open reading frames (ORFs) using the Glimmer3 toolkit. Methods based on the publication: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"88nqr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5kj4c","text":"Hannigan, Geoffrey D., et al. \"The Human Skin Double-Stranded DNA Virome: Topographical and Temporal Diversity, Genetic Enrichment, and Dynamic Associations with the Host Microbiome.\" mBio 6.5 (2015): e01578-15.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8phvl","text":"Required Software:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"k646","text":"Glimmer3 v3.02","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5cbn2","text":"Relevant Files","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"74e3i","text":"Output: Virome_ORFs/Contigs_no_block_with_names_glimmer_output_final.fa","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ak1d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cnhpe","text":"After the contigs were generated from the concatenated fasta files, we predicted the locations of the open reading frames (ORFs) and extracted them from the contigs using the Glimmer3 toolkit. First we generated the needed directories and copied the contig files over to a new directory.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":10,"length":22}],"data":[]},{"key":"27deh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"am4lj","text":"We then built an Interpolated Context Model (ICM), used it to predict the open reading frames, and extracted the open reading frames from the contig sequences. Finally we removed the block fasta format of the ORFs because this can interfere with some of our downstream analyses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"2D17A69643E24AC69B7641C96021706B","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/Script-P2-Contig-Assembly-ed9ba96"}}}}
steps: Generate the needed directories. mkdir ./glimmer3mkdir ./glimmer3/contigsmkdir ./glimmer3/output mkdir ./glimmer3 mkdir ./glimmer3/contigs mkdir ./glimmer3/output
Copy the contigs file (non-block format) to a new directory. cp ./ray_contigs_from_total_cat_pairs/Contigs_no_block_with_names.fasta ./glimmer3/contigs

Perform glimmer extraction. Glimmer3 Toolkit 3.02 Unix The Institute for Genomic Research (TIGR) build-icm ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names.icm " ] then seqname=${line#'>'} else orf="$seqname.${line%%' '*}" coords="${line#*' '}" echo -e "$orf\t$seqname\t$coords" fi done > ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names_glimmer_output.predict.formatted echo 'Performing multi-extract...' multi-extract -l 100 --nostop ./glimmer3/contigs/Contigs_no_block_with_names.fasta ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names_glimmer_output.predict.formatted > ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names_glimmer_output.genes" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="83267FEC2665450B9BF8659625AA59BD" > build-icm ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names.icm < ./glimmer3/contigs/Contigs_no_block_with_names.fasta echo 'Running glimmer...' glimmer3 -g 100 ./glimmer3/contigs/Contigs_no_block_with_names.fasta ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names.icm ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names_glimmer_output cat ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names_glimmer_output.predict | while read line; do if [ "${line:0:1}" == ">" ] then seqname=${line#'>'} else orf="$seqname.${line%%' '*}" coords="${line#*' '}" echo -e "$orf\t$seqname\t$coords" fi done > ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names_glimmer_output.predict.formatted echo 'Performing multi-extract...' multi-extract -l 100 --nostop ./glimmer3/contigs/Contigs_no_block_with_names.fasta ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names_glimmer_output.predict.formatted > ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names_glimmer_output.genes
creator: Geoffrey D Hannigan
